clusters.title: 2 U.S. service members killed, 1 missing following Iranian strike on Jordan
clusters.neutral_headline: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
bullet_summary: US Central Command reported its first troop deaths due to Iranian fire when two service members were killed and an another missing in an attack on a base in Jordan
The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait
US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”

items.title: 2 U.S. service members killed, 1 missing following Iranian strike on Jordan
items.standfirst: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran Saturday evening at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. service members.
items.content: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran Saturday evening at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. service members.

items.content: In addition to the two U.S. service members killed, another is currently missing in action, U.S. Central Command said on Saturday.

items.title: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
items.standfirst: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.
items.content: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

items.title: Khamenei threatens ‘unforgettable lessons’, two US troops killed in Iranian attack
items.standfirst: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Majotaba Khamenei on Saturday vowed to teach the US "unforgettable lessons" in a written message broadcast on state TV. US Central Command reported its first troop deaths due to Iranian fire when two service members were killed and an another missing in an attack on a base in Jordan.
items.content: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Majotaba Khamenei on Saturday vowed to teach the US "unforgettable lessons" in a written message broadcast on state TV. US Central Command reported its first troop deaths due to Iranian fire when two service members were killed and an another missing in an attack on a base in Jordan.

items.title: Two US troops killed and one missing in Jordan after Iranian attacks
items.standfirst: Sirens sound in Bahrain and Kuwait accuses Tehran of targeting civilian sites and infrastructure as Iran strikes US alliesTwo US troops were killed and one remains missing in Jordan after Iran launched a wave of attacks against US allies in the Middle East.Iran’s attacks came as the renewal of US strikes on Iran entered a second week and fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz. items.title: U.S. fires on Iran after attack in Jordan leaves 2 troops killed, 1 missing
items.standfirst: Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.
items.content: Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)

items.title: US military says two service members killed in Iranian strike in Jordan
items.standfirst: US and Iran have stepped up attacks in the last week, prompting alarm about return to full-scale war across the region.
items.content: US and Iran have stepped up attacks in the last week, prompting alarm about return to full-scale war across the region.

items.title: Two U.S. service members killed in Jordan and another is missing after Iranian strikes, military says
items.standfirst: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.
items.content: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.

items.title: 🔴 Middle East live: Two US service members in Jordan killed, one missing in action
Gulf council blasts "war crimes" by Iran
items.standfirst: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait. The Gulf Cooperation Council has condemned Tehran's attacks on civilian sites, describing them as war crimes.
items.content: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait. The Gulf Cooperation Council has condemned Tehran's attacks on civilian sites, describing them as war crimes.

items.title: Power, desalination plants hit in Kuwait
items.standfirst: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait. The Gulf Cooperation Council has condemned Tehran's attacks on civilian sites, describing them as war crimes. Clemence Waller has the latest.
items.content: The United States and Iran continued exchanging strikes on Saturday as tensions over control of the Strait of Hormuz escalated. US Central Command carried out a seventh consecutive night of attacks, targeting military infrastructure and maritime capabilities, while Iran launched strikes on neighbouring Gulf states. The most significant damage was reported at a water desalination plant in Kuwait. The Gulf Cooperation Council has condemned Tehran's attacks on civilian sites, describing them as war crimes. Clemence Waller has the latest.

items.title: US launches seventh night of Iran strikes as Hormuz conflict escalates
items.standfirst: Central Command says attacks were designed to ‘continue degrading Iranian military capabilities’The US military said it had launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran on Friday night as fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz.US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”. Continue reading...

items.title: The U.S. and Iran blow past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
items.standfirst: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.
items.content: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/AP Photo)

items.title: U.S. completes latest round of strikes on Iran’s military sites as Hormuz tensions climb
items.standfirst: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran late Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran late Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.

clusters.title: Men's mile record broken for first time since 1999
clusters.neutral_headline: UK’s Josh Kerr smashes longstanding world mile record in London
bullet_summary: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday
Reported by CBS News, NPR News, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Men's mile record broken for first time since 1999
items.standfirst: British runner Josh Kerr ran the mile in 3 minutes, 42.66 seconds on Saturday, breaking a record that has stood since 1999.
items.content: British runner Josh Kerr ran the mile in 3 minutes, 42.66 seconds on Saturday, breaking a record that has stood since 1999.

items.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
items.standfirst: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.
items.content: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.(Image credit: Harry Murphy)

items.title: UK’s Josh Kerr smashes longstanding world mile record in London
items.standfirst: Kerr ran a 3:42.66 mile at the London Diamond League, smashing Hicham El Guerrouj’s world record by 0.47 seconds.
items.content: Kerr ran a 3:42.66 mile at the London Diamond League, smashing Hicham El Guerrouj’s world record by 0.47 seconds.

items.title: Josh Kerr smashes men’s mile world record that stood unbroken since 1999
items.standfirst: Britain's Josh Kerr clocked 3min 42.66sec in the men's mile at a high-octane Diamond League on Saturday, smashing the record set by Morocco's Hicham El Guerrouj in Rome in 1999.
items.content: Britain's Josh Kerr clocked 3min 42.66sec in the men's mile at a high-octane Diamond League on Saturday, smashing the record set by Morocco's Hicham El Guerrouj in Rome in 1999.

items.title: Britain's Kerr breaks 27-year mile world record in London
items.standfirst: Great Britain's Josh Kerr smashes the long-standing men's mile world record in a spectacular performance at the London Diamond League.
items.content: Great Britain's Josh Kerr smashes the long-standing men's mile world record in a spectacular performance at the London Diamond League.

items.title: Josh Kerr breaks 27-year-old mile record in front of packed London stadium
items.standfirst: British runner Josh Kerr has broken the 27-year-old world record for the mile in front of a sell-out crowd in London.
items.content: British runner Josh Kerr has broken the 27-year-old world record for the mile in front of a sell-out crowd in London.

bullet_summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place
Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, Sky News World and others

items.title: Cockroach Party protest intensifies after Wangchuk forcibly hospitalised
items.standfirst: Protest by India's Cockroach Janta Party has intensified after activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly hospitalised.
items.content: Protest by India's Cockroach Janta Party has intensified after activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly hospitalised.

items.title: Indian police shift Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike to hospital against his wish
items.standfirst: Indian police on Saturday forcibly moved environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital after his health deteriorated during a weeks-long hunger strike in New Delhi. Wangchuk, a prominent activist tied to the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party, is seeking education reforms. His family has denounced the “lack of transparency" and trust in Indian authorities.
items.content: Indian police on Saturday forcibly moved environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital after his health deteriorated during a weeks-long hunger strike in New Delhi. Wangchuk, a prominent activist tied to the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party, is seeking education reforms. His family has denounced the “lack of transparency" and trust in Indian authorities.

items.title: Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'
items.standfirst: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.
items.content: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.

items.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
items.standfirst: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.
items.content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.

clusters.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
clusters.neutral_headline: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, France24

items.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
items.standfirst: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.
items.content: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.

items.title: Germany’s CDU parliamentary leader resigns after using surrogacy to become parent
items.standfirst: Jens Spahn had criticised ‘rented wombs’ and his party is strongly opposed to surrogacy, which is banned in GermanyA senior German politician and ally of the chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has resigned as parliamentary group leader of the Christian Democrat (CDU) party after he and his husband used a surrogate mother to become parents, a practice he has criticised in the past and his party is vehemently opposed to.Surrogacy is banned in Germany, a policy Jens Spahn refused to relax when he was health minister in 2020, so he and his husband, Daniel Funke, used a surrogate mother in the US. Continue reading...
items.content: Jens Spahn had criticised ‘rented wombs’ and his party is strongly opposed to surrogacy, which is banned in GermanyA senior German politician and ally of the chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has resigned as parliamentary group leader of the Christian Democrat (CDU) party after he and his husband used a surrogate mother to become parents, a practice he has criticised in the past and his party is vehemently opposed to.Surrogacy is banned in Germany, a policy Jens Spahn refused to relax when he was health minister in 2020, so he and his husband, Daniel Funke, used a surrogate mother in the US. Continue reading...

items.title: German conservative parliamentary leader resigns over surrogate baby controversy
items.standfirst: Jens Spahn, the parliamentary head of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz's CDU faction, resigned on Saturday amid mounting pressure over his use of a surrogate mother in the US despite a surrogacy ban in Germany. Spahn and his husband welcomed the child earlier this week, sparking criticisms of hypocrisy and double standards.
items.content: Jens Spahn, the parliamentary head of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz's CDU faction, resigned on Saturday amid mounting pressure over his use of a surrogate mother in the US despite a surrogacy ban in Germany. Spahn and his husband welcomed the child earlier this week, sparking criticisms of hypocrisy and double standards.

items.content: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

items.title: Trump threatens Canada with higher tariffs over wildfire smoke

items.content: President Trump threatened more tariffs on Canada for wildfires that have blanketed large parts of the Midwest and East Coast in smoke in recent days.

clusters.title: Armed groups attack Malian military convoy in Gao region
clusters.neutral_headline: Armed groups attack Malian military convoy in Gao region
bullet_summary: A Malian army convoy was ambushed by rebels in a remote part of the northern Gao region, the Malian military confirmed Saturday

items.title: Armed groups attack Malian military convoy in Gao region
items.standfirst: Mali says counterattack under way as rebels claim they inflicted 'human losses' and 'material damage' on troops.
items.content: Mali says counterattack under way as rebels claim they inflicted 'human losses' and 'material damage' on troops.

items.title: Malian army convoy ambushed by rebels north of Gao
items.standfirst: A Malian army convoy was ambushed by rebels in a remote part of the northern Gao region, the Malian military confirmed Saturday. Regional al Qaeda affiliate JNIM and the separatist Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) claimed responsibility for the attack in separate statements that cited "great human losses".
items.content: A Malian army convoy was ambushed by rebels in a remote part of the northern Gao region, the Malian military confirmed Saturday. Regional al Qaeda affiliate JNIM and the separatist Azawad Liberation Front (FLA) claimed responsibility for the attack in separate statements that cited "great human losses".
